policy_statement: Reduce San Francisco sector-based greenhouse gas emissions by at least 61% below 1990 levels by 2030 and achieve net-zero sector-based emissions by 2040 (at least a 90% reduction below 1990), sequestering any residual emissions through nature-based solutions.
verbatim_text:
By integrating these four climate justice commitments, the CAP proposes two ambitious and achievable climate emission reduction targets:
- An interim target of cutting sector-based emissions 61% below 1990 levels by 2030; and
- Net-zero sector-based emissions by 2040, a 90% reduction from 1990 levels

TABLE 5: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION TARGETS
By 2040, achieve net-zero emissions by reducing emissions at least 90%compared to 1990 levels and sequester any residual emissions through nature- based solutions
section_header: THE PATH TO REACH NET-ZERO BY 2040 / EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS
extraction_rationale: Citywide quantified emissions targets with deadlines and defined net-zero mechanism (90% reduction plus sequestration).

policy_statement: Reduce consumption-based emissions by 40% below 1990 levels by 2030 and by 80% below 1990 levels by 2050 (expressed as less than 30 mtCO2e per household by 2030 and less than 10 mtCO2e per household by 2050).
verbatim_text:
San Francisco is determined to reduce the impacts of these outsourced emissions and has set two targets:
- A 40% reduction in consumption-based emissions by 2030
- An 80% reduction in consumption-based emissions by 2050

TABLE 5: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION TARGETS
By 2030, reduce consumption-based emissions to less than 30 mtCO2e per household, equivalent to a 40% reduction compared to 1990 levels
By 2050, reduce consumption-based emissions to less than 10 mtCO2e per household, equivalent to an 80% reduction compared to 1990 levels
section_header: THE PATH TO REACH NET-ZERO BY 2040 / EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS
extraction_rationale: Quantified consumption-based emissions reduction targets with deadlines and per-household metrics.

policy_statement: Supply 100% renewable electricity in San Francisco by 2025 and supply 100% renewable energy (no more fossil fuels) by 2040.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
ENERGY  By 2025, supplying 100% renewable electricity, and by 2040, supplying 100% renewable energy (no more fossil fuels).
extraction_rationale: Clear, dated renewable energy goals stated in the CAP goals table.

policy_statement: Require zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all new buildings by 2021; require zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all large existing commercial buildings by 2035; and achieve zero emissions for all buildings by 2040.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
BUILDINGS  By 2021, requiring zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all new buildings, and by 2035, requiring zero onsite fossil fuel emissions from all large existing commercial buildings.

## BUILDING OPERATIONS
## GOALS:
1) Zero emisisons new construction by 2021
2) All large commerical buildings are zero emissions by 2035
3) All buildings zero emissions by 2040
section_header: EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS / BUILDING OPERATIONS GOALS
extraction_rationale: Mandate-style requirements with deadlines for new buildings, large commercial buildings, and all buildings.

policy_statement: By 2030, achieve at least 80% of trips by low-carbon modes; electrify at least 25% of registered private vehicles by 2030 and 100% by 2040.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
TRANSPORTATION  By 2030, an increase in low-carbon trips to at least 80%of all trips measured and an increase in the level of electrification of vehicles to at least 25%of all private vehicles registered, and by 2040, an increase in the level of electrification of vehicles to 100% of all private vehicles registered.

## TRANSPORTATION & LAND USE
## GOALS:
1) By 2030, 80% of trips taken by low-carbon modes
2) By 2030, at least 25% of all  vehicles registered in SF are electric, reaching 100% by 2040
section_header: EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS / TRANSPORTATION & LAND USE GOALS
extraction_rationale: Quantified transport mode-share and EV adoption targets with explicit deadlines.

policy_statement: Build at least 5,000 new housing units per year with no less than 30% affordable, emphasizing retention and rehabilitation of existing housing.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
HOUSING  Building at least 5,000 new housing units per year with maximum affordability, including not less than 30%affordable units, and with an emphasis on retaining and rehabilitating existing housing.
extraction_rationale: Quantified annual housing production target with affordability floor and stated emphasis on rehab/retention.

policy_statement: By 2030, reduce solid waste generation at least 15% below 2015 levels and reduce waste disposed by incineration or landfill at least 50% below 2015 levels.
verbatim_text:
TABLE 6: 2021 CLIMATE ACTION GOALS
ZEROWASTE  By 2030, a reduction in the generation of solid waste of at least 15% below 2015 levels and a reduction in the amount of solid waste disposed of by incineration or deposited in landfill of at least 50%below 2015 levels.

RESPONSIBLE PRODUCTION & CONSUMPTION
## GOALS:
By 2030:
1) Reduce solid waste generation 15% below 2015 levels
2) Reduce disposal to landfill 50% below 2015 levels
section_header: EMISSIONS TARGETS AND CLIMATE GOALS / RESPONSIBLE PRODUCTION & CONSUMPTION GOALS
extraction_rationale: Quantified waste reduction and landfill disposal targets with deadline.

extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple detailed supporting actions and quantified funding/mileage commitments.

policy_statement: Fund and implement recommendations of the ConnectSF Transit Corridors Study and Muni Forward Plan, including frequent corridors (5-minute service), transit priority measures, signal retiming, stop spacing optimization, and major transit capital projects (TLU.1-1).
verbatim_text:
TLU.1-1 Fund and implement the recommendations of the ConnectSF Transit Corridors Study and Muni Forward Plan, including taking steps to:
- a) Identify and implement key transit corridors for service every 5 minutes or better all day long.

b) Ensure transit on frequent corridors is not delayed by recurring congestion by investing in transit-only lanes, signal management, queue-jump lanes and other transit priority treatments.

- c) Retime traffic lights to minimize signal delay for frequent lines.
- d) Optimize stop spacing on frequent lines to maximize transit ridership.
- e) Advance major transit capital projects, including a new Westside Subway along 19th Avenue and Geary, the Caltrain Downtown Extension, Central Subway extension, and the Link21 new transbay tube.
parent_policy_name: TLU.1: Build a fast and reliable transit system that will be everyone's preferred way to get around.
extraction_rationale: Concrete package of transit service and capital project actions.

extraction_rationale: Specific funding commitment with quantified annual amount.

policy_statement: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.

parent_policy_name: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound development of policy/incentive tools for embodied carbon reduction.

parent_policy_name: RPC.1: Achieve total carbon balance across the buildings and infrastructure sectors.

extraction_rationale: Time-bound best-practice adoption and municipal pilot commitment.

policy_statement: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.

extraction_rationale: Named food system strategy with multiple quantified and time-bound actions.

policy_statement: By 2030, reduce food waste by 50% in alignment with the Pacific Coast Collaborative voluntary commitment by implementing guidelines and recommendations with food retailers/distributors/manufacturers (RPC.2-1).
verbatim_text: RPC.2-1 By 2030, reduce food waste by 50% in alignment with the City's voluntary commitment to the Pacific Coast Collaborative initiative by implementing food waste reduction guidelines and recommendations in partnership with food retail, distributors, and manufacturers.
parent_policy_name: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.

policy_statement: By 2022, continue and scale the Kitchen Zero SF pilot program to track over-purchasing and redirect wasted food to communities in need, including recovered fresh produce for communities with limited access (RPC.2-2).
verbatim_text: RPC.2-2 By 2022, continue implementing and scale the Kitchen Zero SF pilot program, which reduces food waste by tracking over-purchasing by food generators, and redirects otherwise wasted food to communities in need, including providing recovered fresh produce to communities with limited access.
parent_policy_name: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound scale-up of named pilot with equity intent.

policy_statement: By 2024, adopt a Food Waste Prevention and Edible Food Recovery policy and develop compliance/monitoring program and incentives aligned with California SB 1383 (RPC.2-3).
verbatim_text: RPC.2-3 By 2024, adopt a Food Waste Prevention and Edible Food Recovery policy and develop a program and incentives structure for compliance and monitoring in alignment with California's State Bill 1383 food recovery regulations.
parent_policy_name: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
extraction_rationale: Explicit policy adoption with deadline and compliance mechanism.

policy_statement: By 2024, develop guidance for City food procurement aligned with Good Food Purchasing Program core values (including environmental sustainability and regenerative agriculture) (RPC.2-5).
verbatim_text: RPC.2-5 By 2024, develop guidance in partnership with other municipal agencies to implement city procurement of food in alignment with the five core values put forth by the Good Food Purchasing Program (GFPP): developing local economies, improving health, valuing the workforce, considering animal welfare, and environmental sustainability, including regenerative agriculture.
parent_policy_name: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound procurement guidance tied to named program values.

policy_statement: By 2025, SFDPH will ensure Zuckerberg San Francisco General and Laguna Honda Hospitals achieve a 20% reduction in carbon and water footprints via sustainable food purchasing standards aligned with GFPP values (RPC.2-6).

parent_policy_name: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
extraction_rationale: Quantified institutional target with deadline and implementation mechanism.

parent_policy_name: RPC.2: Reduce the carbon footprint of the food system by reducing waste, promoting climate-friendly diets, and getting excess food to communities in need.
extraction_rationale: Time-bound school district procurement/diet/waste actions with equity components.

policy_statement: Create a robust monitoring, evaluation, and reporting (MER) system (including a public-facing dashboard) and report on progress toward CAP targets/goals/strategies annually; formally update the CAP every five years.
verbatim_text:
The CAP must and will be revisited and updated regularly, with a formal update every five years.

To that end, the City will create a robust and accessible monitoring, evaluation, and reporting system to track and review the intended results and real progress of the targets, goals, strategies, and actions.

Ongoing and transparent reporting on key performance indicators, which is to occur annually, will be critical to measure progress against goals and allow for adjustments based on changing conditions. Future CAP updates will occur once every five years and will capture new and ongoing gaps and concerns.
section_header: TRANSPARENCY AND REPORTING / NEXT STEPS FOR IMPLEMENTING THE CAP
extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to create MER system with annual reporting and a defined five-year formal update cycle.

policy_statement: Create an interdepartmental climate finance working group; develop detailed CAP action cost estimates; identify existing funding opportunities and gaps; investigate new/increased taxes; and pursue grants to fund CAP implementation.
verbatim_text:
Overall, recommended next steps include:

1. Create an interdepartmental climate finance working group to assess the economic, social, political, and administrative viability of securing new funding sources.

2. Develop a detailed cost estimate for implementing CAP actions (beyond high-level estimates in the CAP).

3. Identify all opportunities to fund CAP strategies from existing funding sources and approved measures.

4. Assess which CAP strategies are not funded or partially funded to identify funding gaps.

5. Investigate a new tax (carbon tax, food tax) and/or increase existing taxes (sales tax, property tax) as a major contributor to reducing funding gaps.

6. Seek out and apply for relevant federal, state, and local grant opportunities...
extraction_rationale: Specific implementation finance actions enumerated as recommended next steps, including potential new taxes and a formal working group.

policy_statement: The City will take steps to create a new Climate Equity Hub to engage diverse communities in ongoing emissions-reduction and sustainability transition efforts.
verbatim_text:
In 2021, after passionate advocacy from local stakeholders inspired to act by the unfolding climate emergency, the City allocated dedicated funding to develop a detailed analysis of the cost of CAP implementation and identify reliable funding models that would be most successful in San Francisco. It will also take steps to create a new

Climate Equity Hub to ensure San Francisco's diverse communities are engaged in the ongoing efforts to reduce emissions and transition to a more sustainable future.
extraction_rationale: This is an explicit commitment by the City to take action to create a named institution/entity (Climate Equity Hub) with a defined purpose (community engagement in emissions reduction and transition).

policy_statement: San Francisco requires new construction (as of June 2021) to be all-electric, resulting in no on-site fossil fuel combustion starting in 2021 for new buildings in the CAP analysis.
verbatim_text: New construction is required to meet efficiency and safety standards in effect at the time of construction. As of June 2021, new construction in San Francisco is required to be all-electric, so the CAP analysis shows new construction has no on-site fossil fuel combustion starting in 2021.
extraction_rationale: The text states a regulatory requirement (“required to be all-electric”) tied to a date and outcome (no on-site fossil fuel combustion starting in 2021), qualifying as a binding mechanism.

policy_statement: To meet San Francisco's goal of zero emissions by 2040, all existing buildings must be renovated or replaced by 2040.
verbatim_text: As buildings are retrofitted and renovated, the stock of existing buildings decreases in this model. To meet San Francisco's goal of zero emissions by 2040, 100% of existing buildings must be renovated or replaced by 2040.
extraction_rationale: This is a quantified, deadline-based requirement explicitly linked to achieving the City's 2040 zero-emissions goal; it functions as a policy target/condition.

policy_statement: Create an interdepartmental climate finance working group to assess the viability of securing new funding sources and identify targeted funding solutions for CAP implementation across six sectors.
verbatim_text: Create an interdepartmental climate finance working group to assess the economic, social, political, and administrative viability of securing new funding sources and identify targeted funding solutions for CAP implementation across the six sectors.
extraction_rationale: A specific, actionable implementation step (creation of a working group) stated as a recommendation for next steps to fund/finance CAP implementation.

policy_statement: Develop a detailed cost estimate for implementing CAP actions.
verbatim_text: Develop a detailed cost estimate for implementing CAP actions - currently the CAP has only identified strategies and actions needed to meet the City's climate goals, along with and ROM costs
extraction_rationale: Concrete administrative action (produce a detailed cost estimate) tied to CAP implementation funding planning.

policy_statement: Identify opportunities to fund CAP strategies from existing funding sources and approved measures, including accounting for how much of the CAP is already funded through current revenue streams, activities, and bonds.
verbatim_text: Identify all opportunities to fund CAP strategies from existing funding sources and approved measures. Accounting for how much of CAP is already funded through City's current revenue streams, activities, and bonds is imperative to move forward.
extraction_rationale: Specific intervention: conduct a comprehensive identification/accounting exercise to align CAP strategies with existing funding sources.

policy_statement: Assess which CAP strategies are not funded or partially funded to identify funding gaps.
verbatim_text: Assess which CAP strategies are not funded or partially funded to identify funding gaps.
extraction_rationale: Clear directive to perform a gap assessment; actionable planning step to support CAP implementation.

policy_statement: Investigate a new tax (e.g., carbon tax or food tax) and/or increase existing taxes (e.g., sales tax or property tax) to help reduce CAP implementation funding gaps.
verbatim_text: Investigate a new tax (carbon tax, food tax) and/or increase existing taxes (sales tax, property tax) as a major contributor to reducing funding gaps.
extraction_rationale: Identifies a concrete fiscal policy action to pursue (investigate new/increased taxes) explicitly linked to closing CAP funding gaps.

policy_statement: Seek out and apply for relevant federal, state, and local grant opportunities to provide seed funding for implementing CAP strategies and supporting activities (e.g., community engagement or technical analysis).
verbatim_text: Seek out and apply for relevant federal, state, and local grant opportunities which can serve as important seed funding for implementing CAP strategies or other supporting activities such as community engagement or technical analysis.
extraction_rationale: Actionable commitment to pursue external grant funding; includes explicit actions (seek out and apply) and purpose (seed funding for CAP).
